propertyChanges.value: English rock band formed in London in 1964, widely associated with mod culture and known for landmark works including Tommy, Who's Next, and Quadrophenia.
English rock band from Woking, Surrey, formed in 1972 and central to the late-1970s mod revival, blending punk energy with sharp pop songwriting.
English rock band formed in London in 1963 by brothers Ray and Dave Davies, influential in British Invasion, hard rock, and concept-driven British pop albums.
Historic recording studio in St John's Wood, London, opened by EMI in 1931 and closely associated with major British and international recordings.
Recording studio in Hornsey, London, established by members of the Kinks and closely associated with the band's later recording career.
Private recording studio associated with Pete Townshend and named after Eel Pie Island, used for demos and sessions connected to Townshend and the Who orbit.
